title: Ford Family Foundation
html:
Ford Family Foundation

The Roseburg-based Oregon scholarship foundation that more than any single institution made the OSU degree, the Shanghai study abroad, and the India/Nepal / turkey / egypt travel arc financially possible. Yahir applied as a Grants Pass senior in 2020 (2020 11 15 scholarship essays todo lists it among Renaissance, OSU, and JCEF as the four scholarship apps in flight) and the Ford full-ride hit, per 2025 10 20 quality resume. From that point on, "the Ford" becomes a recurring administrative thread: grades to submit, paperwork to coordinate, contact at the foundation named Zane.

The May 2024 todos (2024 05 13 things) document the Ford as the active disbursement mechanism for the Shanghai move — "Coordinate scholarship release with Ford (via Zane) and JCEF (via Quin)", "Sign up for class then drop it" (a known funding-admin loophole), grades-submission. The 2021 Turkey/Egypt bucket-list note (2021 05 04 turkey egypt bucket list) drops the Ford in passing as part of the travel-funding context. By April 2025 the Ford appears at the top of 2025 04 20 people i am indebted to for helping me (under the typo "Bored Family Foundation") — Yahir's own gratitude ledger names it as one of his six fundamental debts, alongside Brenda, Chan Taylor, the Boys & Girls Club, Kim, and his family.

The Ford bracketed conceptually with Boys & Girls Club is the load-bearing reading: half of Yahir's named debts are to institutions that scaffold poor and small-town kids. This is the substrate for the "be a project youth plus donor" aspiration in 2025 05 30 todos — pay the debt forward to the same kind of kid the Ford funded. It also feeds the no runway discipline insistence on staying self-funded with Rankett: not wanting to owe anyone else what he already owes the Ford.

Source summaries

  • 2020 11 15 scholarship essays todo
  • 2021 05 04 turkey egypt bucket list
  • 2024 05 13 things
  • 2025 04 20 people i am indebted to for helping me
